title: Cicerbita villicaulis
taxonomy: Cicerbita villicaulis is classified within the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it among the subfamily Cichorioideae, which includes other plants such as lettuce, chicory, and endive. Further classification of Cicerbita villicaulis places it within the tribe Cichorieae, a group of plants characterized by their leafy rosettes and small, composite flowers. The genus Cicerbita itself is a small group of plants native to the Mediterranean region. Cicerbita villicaulis is a species within this genus.

intro: Cicerbita villicaulis, commonly known as roughstem milfoil, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, gout, and other inflammatory conditions. Cicerbita villicaulis contains bioactive constituents such as sesquiterpene lactones, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, roughstem milfoil is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to create remedies for various health conditions.
